entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
As students begin their adventures with books, we stir their imaginations with fairy tales from around the world.

This topic is included in the following course(s): Literature: Grade 1

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: Suitable for hungry kindergarteners as well.

rows.label: Fairy Tales
rows.books: Around the World in 80 Tales||Tales from Grimm

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Literature: Grade 1
☐ As desired, plan for a read-aloud time together. Suggestions are provided in the Community Read Aloud Quick Link, but local favorites are highly recommended whenever possible.

blocks.text:
• Read through the lesson and gather any materials or find any necessary links. 

• If you have not already read the book, you may want to read the selected pages in advance.

• Connect back to the previous lesson. Ask students to give a summary of what they remember. (Full narration is not necessary.)

• If students have difficulty recalling, teachers might try showing them a picture from the reading, sharing something that the teacher remembers, or prompting them with the first sentence of the last reading. This will often refresh their memory enough to allow them to recall.

• If students still cannot recall anything from the last reading, then teachers might consider continuing anyway (with a shorter reading this time) or repeating the last lesson, depending on the reading and the nature of the book.

• Optional: use a picture, map, or engaging question to pique the student's interest in the day's lesson. 

• If necessary, you may talk about one or two unfamiliar words that will be crucial to understanding.

• Read the day's passage out loud. When reading to pre-readers, point to the words as you read, even if it seems like they aren't paying attention.

• Move at the student’s pace for the lesson time rather than a particular number of pages. It is vitally important that teachers not try to ‘force feed’ a student more than they can consume in one sitting.

• Students who have a difficult time sitting still or listening may benefit from a fidget of their choice, such as a piece of putty or clay or an afghan; flexible positioning, such as a wobble seat or inverted sitting; or sensory modulation, such as a weighted vest or blanket or adjustment of light, temperature, or sound.

• Have students retell the passage orally.

• Alternatively, learners may use words, pictures, Legos, dolls, etc., to process and convey ideas in the manner most natural to them.  

• Teachers may take turns to model.

• Narration is a complex skill requiring the coordination of several parts of the brain. If a student struggles to narrate in general, with a specific subject or book, or on one day but not others, then this is an opportunity to learn. Observe when they struggle with this or any other activity today. Notice facial expressions and body language; listen to what they say about why it is hard or how they feel about it. Take note of these observations for later, tell them that you see how hard they are working, and then offer whatever support they need to end on a positive note. If necessary, take a break for the day.

• Give the student an opportunity to share thoughts on the lesson and any connections made.  

• To stimulate discussion, you could ask them what they thought about the passage or ask an open-ended question.

lessons.title: 15m Fairy Tales - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Hansel and Gretel
⍞ Materials: Tales from Grimm

PREP: Read "Introduction" p.vii-xiii

➜ INTRO
Each week, when we read from our books, our imaginations will take us to some faraway places and introduce us to interesting characters.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.3-10 "In a little" - "all, all up."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER TIP
This version of Grimm's fairy tales is written in beautiful English that is simple enough for some students to begin reading it themselves. You may want to let your student(s) take a turn reading a sentence or a page as they grow in their reading abilities.

lessons.title: 15m Fairy Tales - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
Hansel and Gretel
⍞ Materials: Tales from Grimm

➜ INTRO
Do you remember what was happening to Gretel in this picture?
⍞ Image Resource: Tales from Grimm "Gretel and Hansel Kneeling" p.7

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.10-18 "The two unhappy" - "not get fat."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview for sensitive content p.17

lessons.title: 15m Fairy Tales - Lesson 3
lessons.body:
Hansel and Gretel
⍞ Materials: Tales from Grimm

➜ RECAP
Where did we end last time? 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.18-24 "When four weeks" - "happily ever after."
lessons.teacherNotes:
• ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

★ TEACHER TIP
Students should also be encouraged to narrate using Legos, puppets, or by acting out the scene.

lessons.title: 15m Fairy Tales - Lesson 4
lessons.body:
Cat and Mouse Keep House
⍞ Materials: Tales from Grimm

➜ INTRO
We're starting a new story today called Cat and Mouse Keep House. What do you think it will be about?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.27-35 "A tawny yellow" - "in this world!"
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Fairy Tales - Lesson 5
lessons.body:
Stories from Canada and the Sioux
⍞ Materials: Around the World in 80 Tales

➜ INTRO
Grimm's tales are mostly from Germany. Let's read some fairy tales from other places! These tales are a bit shorter, so we'll read several. 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.13-14 "The White Bear"
p.15-17 "The Three Spells"
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
For the 80 Tales book, 2-3 stories are usually assigned. Depending on your student(s), you may want to stop and narrate after each. Or you could read all the stories and ask your student to pick one to narrate at length.

lessons.title: 15m Fairy Tales - Lesson 6
lessons.body:
Six Servants
⍞ Materials: Tales from Grimm

➜ INTRO
This story will introduce us to a Prince and six unusual men with unique abilities.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.39-51 "In days of" - "will be harder."

lessons.title: 15m Fairy Tales - Lesson 7
lessons.body:
Six Servants
⍞ Materials: Tales from Grimm

➜ RECAP
Where did we stop last time? What do you think the next test will be?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.51-61 "I am ready," - "happiness ever after."
lessons.teacherNotes:
• ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

★ TEACHER TIP
Students should also be encouraged to narrate using Legos, puppets, or by acting out the scene.

lessons.title: 15m Fairy Tales - Lesson 9
lessons.body:
Spindle, Shuttle, and Needle
⍞ Materials: Tales from Grimm

➜ INTRO
We may need help to understand the title of our story. Spindles and shuttles are used in weaving.
∞ Image Link: Spindles 
∞ Video Link: Weaving Shuttle Breakdown

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.65-73 "At the edge" - "poor girl's home."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.body:

⍞ Materials: Tales from Grimm

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.77-84 "Once there was" - "a fine carriage."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Fairy Tales - Lesson 11
lessons.body:
The Musicians of Bremen
⍞ Materials: Tales from Grimm

➜ INTRO
This tale originates from Bremen, a town in Germany. In fact, there is a statue there dedicated to the four animal musicians.
∞ Image Link: Town Musicians of Bremen Statue

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.87-97 "An old, old" - "ease and comfort."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER TIP
As you read tales from around the world, consider finding the places on a map and casually adding more about these places "by the way." Look up extra images, cook food from those locations, etc., as students are interested.

• ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Fairy Tales - Lesson 13
lessons.body:
Cinderella
⍞ Materials: Tales from Grimm

➜ INTRO
Did you know that the Cinderella story can be found in other countries and languages around the world? (See Extra Helpings for suggestions.) You may be surprised to find some new characters in this retelling.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.101-110 "A rich man" - "of her anywhere."

lessons.title: 15m Fairy Tales - Lesson 14
lessons.body:
Cinderella
⍞ Materials: Tales from Grimm

➜ INTRO
What do you think about Cinderella’s stepsisters?
⍞ Image Resource: Tales from Grimm "The Two Haughty Sisters" p.105

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.110-120 "In the meantime" - "happy life together."
lessons.teacherNotes:
• ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

Students should also be encouraged to narrate using Legos, puppets, or by acting out the scene.

terms.content: · Tales from Grimm or Around the World in 80 Tales: Tell about or illustrate one of the tales you enjoyed this term.
